title: Low
review: Very obvious influence from artists like Kraftwerk. Its a very ambitious project by David and i absolutely love it.

title: Mona Lisa Overdrive
review: One of their best albums. Filled with an assortment of different sounds and masterful production. Imai creates an amazing atmosphere with the electronic sounds. Some tracks, like 'Girl', sound very pop while others, such as 'Zangai', are very metal-esque
highlights: Zangai, Girl, Mona Lisa, Sid Vicious on The Beach

title: Toxicity
review: Heavy, raw, and unfiltered. Serj Tankian and Daron Malakian's versatility really shows on this record. Every member of the band does great here and this is true genre-defining rock of the early 2000s. A must listen.
highlights: Chop Suey, Toxicity, Aerials, Deer Dance

title: White Pony
review: Really awesome albums with a dynamic sound. Heavy and raw, but other times ethereal and dreamy. A must listen.
highlights: Passenger, Knife Party, Change (In the House of Flies), Digital Bath

title: Doolittle
review: A seminal alternative rock album with dynamic shifts and surreal lyrics.
highlights: Debaser, Wave of Mutilation, Monkey Gone to Heaven

title: The Downward Spiral
review: Industrial and emotionally brutal; a defining concept album.
highlights: March of the Pigs, Closer, Hurt

title: In Rainbows
review: Warm, intricate, and sonically adventurous; a late-2000s high point.

title: Kid A
review: Avant-garde breakthrough; sparse melodies and cinematic ambience.
highlights: Everything In Its Right Place, How to Disappear Completely

title: Nevermind
review: Defining moment of the grunge era; raw energy and hooks.
highlights: Smells Like Teen Spirit, Come as You Are

title: Illmatic
review: Timeless hip-hop storytelling with jazzy production.
highlights: N.Y. State of Mind, It Ain't Hard to Tell

title: Enter the Wu-Tang (36 Chambers)
review: Raw, gritty, iconic production; a cornerstone of hip-hop.
highlights: Protect Ya Neck, C.R.E.A.M., Wu-Tang Clan Ain't Nothin' ta F' Wit
